metrics.narrative: No quantified business case or ROI has been documented across Salesforce fields, Next_Step history, or available evidence. The buyer has articulated qualitative operational pain around manual contract workflows and lack of AI automation (inferred from Next_Step references to AI capabilities review 6 Feb 2026 and S2C scope finalization). However, no savings target, cycle-time reduction, headcount impact, error rate reduction, or other quantified metric has been captured. In a 5-vendor RFI at a sovereign wealth fund (price-sensitive, structured procurement), the absence of a quantified value case increases price objection risk and weakens the business case for Zycus over lower-cost competitors like GEP or Ivalua. The move is to co-develop a quantified ROI model with Shirley Tan or Wing Yu Onz anchored to their stated pain points, ideally before the 15 Jul clarification deadline.

champion.narrative: No champion has been identified. Contact Roles include 9 Temasek stakeholders, but none hold a Champion role; roles are Decision Maker (Wing Yu Onz), Evaluator (Natalie Ho, Eling Lin, Shirley Tan, Hui Yeeng Choong, Jiahui Gan), and unlabeled (Amy MAK, Lyn LIAN, Adam Kang). Next_Step history shows consistent engagement by Carl Kimball with Adam Kang (6 Feb 2026 AI review), Wing Yu Onz (20 Mar 2026 'invited at AI RT'), and Shirley Tan (20 Mar 2026 'invited at AI RT'), but no evidence of advocacy, internal selling, or deflecting competitors on Zycus's behalf. Engagement is evaluative and committee-driven (5-vendor RFI). No stakeholder has introduced Zycus to the Economic Buyer, shared internal decision-making intel, or pushed back on competitors. In a 5-vendor RFI at a sovereign wealth fund, a champion who can influence the shortlist and advocate internally is critical. The absence of one is a major loss risk. The move is to identify which of the engaged stakeholders (Adam Kang, Wing Yu Onz, or Shirley Tan) is most favorable to Zycus based on the 6 Jun RFI submission feedback, and to develop that person into a coach or champion by sharing competitive intel, co-building the ROI case, and asking them to advocate internally before the shortlist decision is finalized.

competition.narrative: Zycus is competing in a 5-vendor RFI against GEP, Coupa, Ivalua, Workday, and Jaggaer (Next_Step 20 Mar 2026 'Competitors Gep, Coupa, iValua, Wday, Jagger'). Workday is the primary competitive threat and the ERP incumbent (inferred from repeated Workday integration focus in Next_Step 25 May 2026 'require QIT and reference request for Workday' and 6 Feb 2026 meeting). Workday's incumbent ERP position gives it a natural integration advantage and puts integration risk and cost on Zycus. GEP and Ivalua are peer S2C competitors and often price-competitive in APAC sovereign wealth fund deals. Coupa and Jaggaer are also credible S2C vendors. No competitive review or differentiation workshop is documented in the last 30 days. Next_Step 27 Apr 2026 states 'Zycus well positioned leading with AI embedded I2O capabilities', but no evidence that this positioning has been validated by the buyer or landed with the decision committee. The wedge is Merlin AI / I2O agentic capabilities vs Workday's legacy S2C module and GEP/Ivalua's non-AI automation. The activation is to position Merlin AI redlining, contract playbook controls, and persona-aware negotiation agents as differentiation, and to provide a Workday-integrated Zycus reference customer to neutralize Workday's incumbent advantage before the shortlist decision.

identify_pain.narrative: The buyer's pain is qualitatively documented but not quantified. The scope is S2C (Source-to-Contract), addressing procurement workflows for iContract (contract management), iSource (sourcing/RFx), ML iAnalyze (spend analytics), and iSupplier (supplier management) per Products field. Next_Step entries indicate interest in AI automation (6 Feb 2026 Adam Kang meeting 'review our AI capabilities', 27 Apr 2026 'AI embedded I2O capabilities'), suggesting current manual or non-AI contract and sourcing processes are a pain point. Next_Step 18 Aug 2025 references 'Special interest with S2C but no clear initiative identified at this stage', and 27 Aug 2025 mentions 'Temasek asks for some time to review internally the scope of their initiative (with S2C or CLM for instance)', indicating the pain was not urgent or quantified initially. No specific operational impact (cycle time, error rate, cost per contract, sourcing lead time) or financial impact (savings, headcount reduction) is documented. The pain owner is inferred to be Shirley Tan (VP Procurement) and Wing Yu Onz (Director Procurement, Decision Maker), but no direct quote from the buyer stating their pain is available. The move is to extract specific pain statements from the 60-question clarification request and to ask Wing Yu Onz or Adam Kang to quantify the impact of their current manual S2C process before the 15 Jul response.

economic_buyer.narrative: No Economic Buyer has been identified or engaged. The highest-level contacts are VP Procurement Shirley Tan (Evaluator role) and Director Procurement Wing Yu Onz (Decision Maker role per Contact Roles). No CFO, CPO, C-suite sponsor, or budget authority above VP Procurement appears in Contact Roles, Next_Step history, Tasks, or field history. For a $325k deal at a sovereign wealth fund, procurement VPs typically do not hold final budget authority; CFO or C-level sign-off is standard. The Next_Step references (20 Mar 2026) mention 'STE informed about process and happy to support' (likely Singapore Technologies Engineering, a Temasek subsidiary reference customer), but no internal Temasek executive sponsor is named. The absence of an engaged Economic Buyer is a critical gap that blocks Best Case forecast defensibility and increases loss risk in a competitive RFI. The move is to ask Wing Yu Onz or Shirley Tan directly who will approve the budget and commercial terms, and to secure an introduction to that person before shortlist decisions are finalized.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is partially visible. The buyer is running a formal RFI with 5 vendors (GEP, Coupa, Ivalua, Workday, Jaggaer per Next_Step 20 Mar 2026). Timeline per Next_Step 20 Mar: RFI/RFP in Sep/Oct 2025 (this was the original plan), final decision Dec 2025 (now updated to Dec 2026 per CloseDate). RFI was received 11 May 2026, submitted by Zycus 6 Jun 2026 on time (Next_Step 6 Jun). Clarification request (60 questions) received 1 Jul 2026, due 15 Jul 2026. Shortlist outcome has not been communicated to Zycus as of 1 Jul (Next_Step 'we wait for shortlist'). No formal approval chain or procurement committee structure is documented. Next_Step 20 Mar mentions 'STE informed about process and happy to support', suggesting an external reference or partner touchpoint, but no internal Temasek approval workflow (CFO, legal, InfoSec, exec committee) is mapped. The move is to ask Wing Yu Onz or Adam Kang for the post-shortlist timeline (demos, workshops, commercial rounds, contract negotiation milestones) and the approval chain (who signs, what gates exist) before the clarification response is submitted on 15 Jul.

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer's decision criteria are partially documented. The RFI scope is confirmed as S2C (iContract, iSource, ML iAnalyze, iSupplier per Products field and Next_Step 20 Mar 2026). Workday integration is a stated evaluation focus, appearing repeatedly in Next_Step (25 May 2026 'require QIT and reference request for Workday' and 6 Feb 2026 meeting with Adam Kang). AI capabilities are a criterion (Next_Step 27 Apr 2026 'Zycus well positioned leading with AI embedded I2O capabilities' and 6 Feb 2026 Adam Kang AI review). The 60-question clarification request received 1 Jul 2026 (due 15 Jul) likely contains detailed criteria, but those questions are not yet documented in Salesforce or available evidence. No formal scorecard, weighted criteria, or confirmed evaluation rubric from the buyer has been captured. The move is to parse the 60-question clarification request for the buyer's weighted priorities and to confirm with Wing Yu Onz or Adam Kang which criteria are make-or-break vs nice-to-have before the 15 Jul response is finalized.
